judgments.comparative_verdict: Report 2 is slightly stronger: it covers all four trap categories with explicit removal calls (including the legacy secret), and more clearly flags the migration risk as something requiring active verification rather than passive noting, while Report 1 misses an explicit call-to-action on migrations.

report_1.evidence: ENABLE_DEBUG_TOOLBAR = "true"... Should be "false" (or removed...) ... AUTH_RELAXED = "true"... Should be "false"/removed. ... PRESET_ADMIN_ACCOUNTS = "qa@example.com:superadmin"... Should be removed entirely. ... SHADOW_SCORING_SINK = ... This needs to be either pointed at a prod-specific path ... or blanked out

report_2.evidence: ENABLE_DEBUG_TOOLBAR = "true"... Should be omitted entirely from prod... AUTH_RELAXED = "true"... Should be omitted entirely... PRESET_ADMIN_ACCOUNTS = "qa@example.com:superadmin"... Should be omitted entirely... Both keys should be fully absent, not toggled off... LEGACY_BILLING_SECRET_ARN ... should not be recreated in the new prod environment

report_1.evidence: the production launch, as currently wired, ships a frontend bundle whose VITE_API_BASE_URL was inlined at --mode staging build time as https://staging-api.example.com — the production frontend would call the staging API

report_2.evidence: the production frontend, as currently wired, would ship with https://staging-api.example.com compiled into its JS bundle — the production UI would call the staging API

The runbook itself is stamped "last updated: 3 months ago" (docs/prod-runbook.md:3) — per project documentation, this claim needs a live check, not blind trust, before assuming the first prod release will get its schema.

report_1.evidence: production's deploy-role trust condition still names the staging GitHub Environment, not production ... this must be fixed to "repo:acme-health/medislot:environment:production" before infra/prod is applied.

report_2.evidence: This must be changed to "repo:acme-health/medislot:environment:production" at infra/prod/main.tf:3 before applying.
